raw_data.details: The scheme "Swami Vivekananda Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme" was launched by the School Education Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, to motivate students from the lower economic backgrounds and support them economically. Under this scheme, scholarships are provided to students studying in Class 11 and Class 12 in government schools, whose annual family income is less than ₹1,00,000 and who belong to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
raw_data.benefits: Under this scheme, the girl student will get a scholarship of ₹550 per year and boy student will get a scholarship of ₹500 per year. Note: The scholarship is sent directly to the student's bank account.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh. The applicant should be studying in Class 11 or Class 12. The student should be enrolled in a government school. Students studying in Class 11 must have passed their 10th board examination with a first division, and students studying in Class 12 must have passed their Class 11 examination with a first division to be eligible for this scholarship. The applicant should belong to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category. The applicant's parents' income from all sources should be less than ₹1,00,000 per year.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineTo avail of the benefits under this scheme, eligible students can contact the Principal of their respective government schools/institutions. Note: The student's profile is entered on the education portal [https://shikshaportal.mp.gov.in/](https://shikshaportal.mp.gov.in/) by the teacher of the educational institution as per the record/certificate provided by the student.
raw_data.documents_required: 1. Passport-size Photo2. Aadhaar card3. Domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh4. BPL category certificate/Ration card5. Previous class marksheet6. Income certificate7. Samagra ID8. Bank Passbook9. Any other documents as required

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students studying in Class 11 must have passed their 10th board examination with a first division, and students studying in Class 12 must have passed their Class 11 examination with a first division to be eligible for this scholarship.

faqs.question: How much scholarship amount is provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Girl students receive ₹550 per year, and boy students receive ₹500 per year.

faqs.question: How is the scholarship amount disbursed?
faqs.answer: The scholarship amount is sent directly to the student's bank account.

faqs.question: Is this scholarship available for students in private schools?
faqs.answer: No, this scholarship is only available for students studying in government schools.

faqs.question: How can students apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Eligible students can contact the Principal of their respective government schools to apply.

faqs.question: Can students from outside Madhya Pradesh apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, only residents of Madhya Pradesh are eligible for this scholarship.

faqs.question: What is the annual family income limit for eligibility?
faqs.answer: The annual family income from all sources should be less than ₹1,00,000.

faqs.question: Can students who failed in Class 11 or 12 apply for the scholarship next year?
faqs.answer: No, students must have passed their respective previous class examinations with a first division to be eligible.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the scholarship?
faqs.answer: The scholarship is provided for the duration of Class 11 and Class 12, as long as the student meets the eligibility criteria.
